feature.name: Speculation rules: form_submission field
feature.creator: robertlin@chromium.org
feature.summary:
This extends speculation rules syntax to allow developers to specify the form_submission field for prerender.

This field directs the browser to prepare the prerender as a form submission, so that it can be activated by real form submission navigations. Examples include a simple search form which results in a /search?q=XXX GET request navigation, support of which has been requested by web developers.

intake.finding:
ChromeStatus record (created 2026-02-10 by robertlin@chromium.org). This extends speculation rules syntax to allow developers to specify the form_submission field for prerender.

This field directs the browser to prepare the prerender as a form submission, so that it can be activated by real form submission navigations. Examples include a simple search form which results in a /search?q=XXX GET request navigation, support of which has been requested by web developers. Category: Performance. Blink component: Internals>Preload>Prerender. Stars: 99.

release.finding: Shipped in Chrome 146 (In developer trial (Behind a flag)).

adoption.finding: The WebDX/WebFeature counter 'SpeculationRules' (ChromeStatus timeline, bucket_id 317) measured 12.4657% of measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads on 2026-07-20. Tracked since 2025-04-22 with 455 data points. Mapping confidence: declared-family.
adoption.limitations: Counter measures the SpeculationRules family/property, not this specific feature. Does not isolate the Chrome 146 increment.

deprecation.finding: This is a capability addition or behavior change, not a deprecation.
